en.extract:
The African grey flycatcher, grayish flycatcher, or large flycatcher (Bradornis microrhynchus) is a passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae that occurs in parts of East Africa.


Taxonomy

The African grey flycatcher was previously placed in the genus Melaenornis but was moved to Bradornis based on the results of a molecular phylogenetic study published in 2010. The subspecies B. m. pumilus is sometimes considered a full species, the Ethiopian grey flycatcher (Bradornis pumilus).


Distribution and habitat
It is found in Ethiopia, Kenya, Somalia, South Sudan, Tanzania, and Uganda.  Its natural habitats are dry savanna and subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.

The '''African grey flycatcher''', '''grayish flycatcher''', or '''large flycatcher''' ('''''Bradornis microrhynchus''''') is a [[passerine]] bird in the Old World flycatcher family [[Muscicapidae]] that occurs in parts of [[East Africa]].

==Taxonomy==
[[File:African Grey Flycatcher 2.jpg|thumb|left|Lake Bogoria - Kenya]]
The African grey flycatcher was previously placed in the [[genus]] ''[[Melaenornis]]'' but was moved to ''Bradornis'' based on the results of a [[molecular phylogenetic]] study published in 2010.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Sangster  | first1=G. | last2=Alström | first2=P. | last3=Forsmark | first3=E. | last4=Olsson | first4=U. | year=2010 | title=Multi-locus phylogenetic analysis of Old World chats and flycatchers reveals extensive paraphyly at family, subfamily and genus level (Aves: Muscicapidae) | journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution | volume=57 | issue=1 | pages=380–392 | doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2010.07.008 | pmid=20656044| bibcode=2010MolPE..57..380S }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | title=Chats, Old World flycatchers | work=World Bird List Version 13.2 | url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/chats/ |publisher=International Ornithologists' Union| access-date=27 November 2023}}</ref> The subspecies ''B. m. pumilus'' is sometimes considered a full species, the Ethiopian grey flycatcher (''Bradornis pumilus'').

== Distribution and habitat==
It is found in [[Ethiopia]], [[Kenya]], [[Somalia]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], and [[Uganda]].  Its natural [[habitat]]s are dry [[savanna]] and subtropical or tropical dry [[shrubland]].
